questions.question: A tennis player wins $\frac{3}{5}$ of her matches. If she plays 15 matches, how many does she win?
A basketball team scores $\frac{2}{3}$ of their shots. If they take 18 shots, how many go in?
A cyclist rides $\frac{1}{4}$ of a 20-mile course. How many miles does she ride?
A runner runs $\frac{3}{8}$ of a 32-mile marathon. How many miles does she run?
A football team wins $\frac{2}{5}$ of their games. If they play 25 games, how many do they win?
A swimmer completes $\frac{1}{6}$ of a 30-lap swim. How many laps does she complete?
A golfer hits $\frac{3}{7}$ of his drives straight. If he hits 21 drives, how many go straight?
A volleyball player spikes $\frac{2}{3}$ of her attacks. If she attempts 24 spikes, how many succeed?
A soccer team has played 10 games and won $\frac{3}{5}$ of them. How many more games must they win to have a $\frac{2}{3}$ win rate for the season, assuming they play a total of 15 games?

A baseball player has a batting average of $\frac{2}{5}$. If he has 20 at-bats, how many hits does he have? If he wants to raise his average to $\frac{1}{2}$, how many more hits must he get in the next 10 at-bats?

teacher_rules: Emphasize the importance of labeling and checking units in mixture problems.
Use visual aids like diagrams or charts to help students understand complex mixtures.
Encourage students to check their work by plugging their answers back into the original equation.
